item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Belated suggestion: One option would be to make clear that whatever is causing this does not solely have opinions that fit in with 21st century legal and moral stances. For instance, by including a couple instances of it making a decision that most people would find repulsive or nonsensical. "Anomalous judicial entity opposes enslaving black people" feels kinda like a moral; "anomalous judicial entity opposes enslaving blacks, farm animals, food crops, and intestinal bacteria" feels a lot more, well, weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with TL333s that you should probably take this in a more "anomalous court that enforces strange rulings" direction; however, the concept is interesting and I like the implication at the end that it overruled its own containment procedures. Reluctant +1, but I'd suggest you put a bit more work into the rulings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, it is. I thought I was spelling it out too much.

I do sort of wish you had run the new entries in the last log through D&C, though.

Agreed. I think plenty of people at the time would have agreed with those views, though. Anyway, any suggestions on how to improve them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just, for an anomalous court that overturns ruling after ruling, I would expect a little less consistency and a lot more "This is not actually how you law" weirdness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't intend it to be "a thing that rights wrongs and fights crime!", more like "a thing that does what it thinks it's right, disregarding due process and other important concepts". But yeah, I didn't explore the negative consequences of this, so I understand where you are coming from.

It doesn't make everyone agree, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite the fact that I agree with a lot of the outcomes here — especially the Pluto one — this is "I wish I had the power to make everyone agree with me", the SCP. Pure wish-fulfillment is not inherently interesting on its own. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the Document History implying what I think it's implying? And the Dred Scott appeal could cause one to think that the Foundation started the Civil War for containment purposes. I like both of these things.

Edit: I do sort of wish you had run the new entries in the last log through D&C, though. The last one in particular is jarring in its phrasing, and these do seem almost cherry-picked to be "things that we, people on the Internet in 2018, would think about decisions that were made in the past". It does take one out of the story a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP in the mainlist. Thank you for your critique, Eskobar. Let's see how long this survives.
